# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Project Overview

FRMX is a browser-based design tool for residential prefab wall panels. The core model is a **layered wall assembly** system — one canonical model produces plan views, 3D views, elevation views, and BOM/reports.

**Key domain concepts**: Wall → Panel → LayerStack + FramingModel. A Panel is a fabrication unit carrying the full layer stack, framing layout, and edge/connection metadata.

**Core Design Principle**: Manual control is paramount. Any auto-generated result (framing layout, panel splits, module placements) must be editable afterward. Overrides are stored as deltas so regeneration can warn on conflicts. The user always retains final say — automation assists, never dictates.

## Architecture

### Monorepo Structure

```
frmx/
├── apps/web/                 # Next.js 15 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS v4
└── packages/
    ├── calculator/           # Imperial feet-inches arithmetic, fractions, roof pitch (foundational)
    ├── core/                 # Types, validation, IDs (nanoid), Zod schemas
    ├── geometry/             # 2D/3D primitives, wall-local frames, polyline utilities
    ├── framing-engine/       # Studs (full/jack/king/cripple), headers, cripples
    ├── panelizer/            # Wall → panels at max-length and opening boundaries
    ├── layers/               # Layer stack presets (2x6 exterior, interior partition)
    ├── modules/              # Opening presets, corner/intersection configs, components
    ├── materials/            # BOM rollup, waste factors, lumber/sheathing catalogs
    ├── reports/              # Panel schedule, cut lists, lumber + sheathing summaries
    └── project-io/           # IndexedDB persistence, .frmx.json export/import
```

**Package dependencies**: `calculator` is foundational — all packages depend on it for dimension math. Other packages form a roughly linear dependency chain: `core` → `geometry` → `framing-engine` → `panelizer` → higher-level features.

### Technology Stack

- **App**: Next.js 15 (App Router), React 19, Tailwind CSS v4, Zustand for state
- **State**: Zustand store with history/undo-redo, selection (wall + panel), view modes
- **2D views**: Canvas 2D with custom dimension rendering (pan/zoom, hit testing, grid)
- **3D**: react-three-fiber + drei + leva (OrbitControls, Environment, Grid, ContactShadows, LEVA GUI)
- **Persistence**: IndexedDB via `@frmx/project-io` + `.frmx.json` export/import
- **Imperial math**: `@frmx/calculator` handles all dimension math — fractions (1/4", 1/8", 1/16"), feet-inches strings ("10'-6 3/4"), roof pitch (rise/run ↔ degrees ↔ percentage)

### Domain Model

```
Project
├── Standards (spacing, lumber grades, max panel size, layer presets)
├── MaterialsCatalog
├── ModuleLibrary
│   ├── OpeningPreset[]     # window bay, door jamb (with RO dimensions)
│   ├── CornerPreset[]       # L-wrap, two-panel + corner stud pack
│   ├── IntersectionPreset[] # L, T, cross connections
│   └── Component[]          # chase, electrical pack, hold-downs
└── Building
    └── Level[]
        ├── Wall[]            # centerline/chain, height, type
        │   ├── Opening[]
        │   └── Panel[]       # ordered along wall
        │       ├── LayerStack[]
        │       ├── FramingModel   # studs, headers, plates, cripples, blocking
        │       ├── ModuleInstance[]
        │       └── Connections[]
        └── Space[] (optional)
```

**FramingModel** is generated by panelizer + framing-engine, then editable in elevation. All changes flow through a command stack enabling full undo/redo.

### 3D View Architecture

Built with `@react-three/fiber` + `@react-three/drei`, loaded via `next/dynamic` with `ssr: false` to avoid React 19 compatibility issues. A mount guard ensures R3F only initializes after React hydration.

Key patterns:
- `Scene.tsx` — thin wrapper with `useEffect` mount guard + `dynamic()` import
- `Scene3D.tsx` — the actual R3F Canvas; cleaned up on unmount via OrbitControls disposal
- `Wall3D.tsx` uses `THREE.InstancedMesh` for studs, tracks disposal via `BuildResult.dispose()`
- LEVA provides in-viewport GUI for floor/ceiling/grid/shadows/camera controls
- Bidirectional sync: 3D reads `selectedWallId` from Zustand store; selection highlights walls

**Critical**: R3F OrbitControls must be disposed on unmount to prevent pointer event capture blocking the 2D canvas.

### Prefab-Specific Rules

- Stud spacing: 16" / 24" OC
- Plate count: single/double top, single bottom
- Max panel dimensions for transport (configurable per plant, default 96")
- Break at openings with configurable offset
- Corner panels: L-wrap vs two-panel + corner stud pack
- Wall intersections: L, T, and cross connections with preset configurations

## Development Commands

```bash
# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Build all packages
npx turbo build

# Run all tests (web)
cd apps/web && npx vitest run

# Run a single test file
cd apps/web && npx vitest run src/path/to/test.ts

# Development server
cd apps/web && pnpm dev

# Build web app
cd apps/web && pnpm build
```

## Build Notes

- **Tailwind v4**: Use `@tailwindcss/postcss` in `postcss.config.js`, NOT `tailwindcss` directly as a plugin
- **tsup external**: Packages with `workspace:*` dependencies must list them as `external` in `tsup.config.ts`
- **ESLint**: Disabled in `.eslintrc.js` — re-enable with proper ESLint 9 flat config when needed

## Performance Targets

60fps orbit with instanced studs; 2D pan/zoom < 16ms frame on M1 laptop. Target: ~80 walls, ~400 panels per building.

## Out of v1 Scope

IFC import, structural engineering sign-off, CNC machine post (CSV only), multi-tenant ERP.
